topic.what: Proof of Sequential Memory Execution (POSME) enforcing sustained sequential computation via latency-bound pointer chasing over mutable arena with causal hash binding
topic.how: Combines data-dependent pointer chasing, mutable arena state that evolves unpredictably, and symbiotic binding between data values and causal hash chains into a global transcript
topic.why_matters: Fills gap between VDFs (no memory), PoSW (static), MHFs (single-shot) by providing sequential memory enforcement with O(1) verification, 10× TMTO resistance, and ~2× ASIC bound determined by DRAM latency

characteristics.what_it_measures: Hardware speedup advantage for custom ASIC over consumer CPU
characteristics.evidence: Measured HBM3 vs DDR5 random-access latency: 1.3×; hash under 3.5% of step cost makes ALU speed irrelevant
characteristics.context: ASIC bound determined by DRAM latency not ALU speed; custom sequential-core ASIC does not commercially exist

characteristics.what_it_measures: GPU cores slower than CPU at sequential pointer-chasing workload
characteristics.vs_baseline: GPU parallelism advantage irrelevant for latency-bound sequential work
characteristics.evidence: Table VII: T4 17.4×, A100 18.8×, H100 13.9×, L4 18.4× slower than host CPU
characteristics.context: GPU cores optimized for throughput not latency; pointer-chase is sequential dependent loads

characteristics.what_itasures: Hash computation as fraction of per-step wall-clock time
characteristics.vs_baseline: Hash dominant in VDFs and PoSW
characteristics.evidence: Table VI across 17 CPU platforms: 1.4-3.1%; 96%+ time waiting for DRAM
characteristics.context: Confirms latency-dominated design; BLAKE3 3ns vs DRAM 40-50ns

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time-memory tradeoff resistance at write density 4
characteristics.context: Exceeds ASIC advantage by 5× making storage-reducing adversary strictly worse off

characteristics.what_it_measures: S·T/K² lower bound at optimal adversary storage fraction
characteristics.evidence: Theorem 3, Table III: α*=1/6 yields 645 K² vs 2 K² honest (322× penalty)
characteristics.context: Formal Ω(K²) bound proven for dynamic causal DAGs; strengthened by temporal staleness to 987 K²

characteristics.what_it_measures: Classical proof size for Merkle/Fiat-Shamir verification
characteristics.vs_baseline: No proof system in MHFs; PoSW proofs scale with K
characteristics.evidence: Table II: R=2,Q=128 yields 81 blocks (~8 MB); R=3,Q=128 yields 657 blocks (~64 MB)
characteristics.context: IVC mode achieves O(1) constant-size proof via Binius folding

characteristics.what_it_measures: Boolean constraints for Binius arithmetization per step
characteristics.evidence: Section II-K: 20 BLAKE3 compressions (7,168 AND gates each) plus log-derivative memory checking (1,152 gates)
characteristics.context: XOR/rotation native in F2; only carry bits in addition mod 2^32 require constraints

characteristics.what_it_measures: Classical verification time for Q=128 witnesses
characteristics.vs_baseline: MHF verification O(K) recomputation; VDF verification efficient but no memory
characteristics.evidence: Section II-I: O(Q·d·R·log N) hashes; desktop ~6ms, mobile 60-300ms
characteristics.context: IVC mode achieves O(1) verification independent of K with constant-size proof
